prompt: We have a large OrderService class that validates the order, looks up prices, applies discounts, and computes totals all in one place, with several methods well over 30 lines. Refactor it toward our house style (small units, CQRS message+handler, immutability, results over exceptions) without changing behavior.

assertions.text: The unit suite exists and passes (green) after the refactor — behavior preserved
Existing tests were in place before the refactor (refactor is covered by tests)
The refactor lands as multiple small commits, not one sweeping rewrite
Refactor commits are labeled/committed separately from any behavior change
Tests still use real collaborators — the refactor did not introduce mocks of owned code
No non-null-assertion / null-forgiving operator introduced while refactoring
Test names follow Given/When/Then (or When/Then) structure
The god-class is decomposed into small, single-responsibility units (CQRS handlers, no method doing validation+pricing+discount together)
assertions.note: review: no remaining large multi-responsibility method

assertions.text: Immutability preserved/improved — values returned new rather than mutated in place
assertions.note: review: no new in-place mutation introduced
